name: Sherwin Williams Emp.
description_short: Scott & White Employees Credit Union (SWECU) is a member-owned credit union serving employees of Baylor Scott and White Health and their families, plus residents and workers in Bell County, Texas.
description_long:
Scott & White Employees Credit Union (SWECU) is a federally-insured, non-profit credit union headquartered in Central Texas that has been serving the healthcare and broader community for decades. The organization is primarily designed to serve employees of Baylor Scott and White Health and their immediate family members, but membership has been expanded to include anyone who resides, works, attends school, owns a business, or worships in Bell County, Texas, making it accessible to a broader regional audience.

SWECU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products typical of mid-sized credit unions. These include deposit accounts (savings and share certificates), auto loans and refinancing (with rates as low as 3.99% APR), home mortgages (30-year fixed options), home equity loans, personal loans, recreational vehicle loans, credit cards, and student loans. The credit union also provides supporting financial tools such as loan calculators, payment portals, and a refinance calculator. Beyond lending, SWECU members can access debit card services, online and mobile banking, check reordering, and courtesy pay features.

What distinguishes SWECU is its integration of professional financial planning services through ClearVista Financial, an independent financial services company with a branch located inside the credit union. This partnership offers members access to retirement planning, estate planning, and college funding strategy services led by Mark Trice, a personal finance consultant with 25+ years of experience. Additionally, SWECU provides the SavvyMoney program, which gives all members free access to their credit score and report with change notifications—a value-added service that supports financial awareness and monitoring.

As a credit union, SWECU operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance protection rather than FDIC coverage, which provides equivalent consumer deposit safety up to $250,000. The organization emphasizes security awareness and actively warns members about caller ID spoofing scams. However, as a membership-based institution with eligibility restrictions (particularly employer affiliation), it may not be accessible to all consumers outside its defined service areas or without qualifying employment or residence.
services: SavvyMoney free credit score and report monitoring
Financial planning (retirement, estate, college planning through ClearVista Financial)
Online and mobile banking with loan payment portal
pros: Low auto loan rates starting at 3.99% APR with terms up to 84 months and up to 125% financing available
Free credit score and credit report access for all members through SavvyMoney program with change alerts
On-site financial planning services through ClearVista Financial covering retirement, estate, and college planning
Competitive first mortgage rates (5.750% / 5.896% APR as advertised) with 30-year fixed options
Expanded membership eligibility beyond employer base to include Bell County residents, workers, and business owners
Comprehensive loan and refinancing options including recreational vehicles, home equity, and student loans
NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits (equivalent to FDIC protection up to $250,000)
cons: Primary membership is restricted to Baylor Scott and White employees, limiting accessibility for most consumers
Unsecured personal loan rates starting at 10.00% APR are higher than many competing lenders
Limited geographic service area (primarily Central Texas / Bell County), making in-person services inaccessible to most of Texas
Website does not prominently display full rate schedules, specific loan terms, or detailed fee structures
No mention of mobile app or modern digital banking features beyond basic online banking portal
best_for: Baylor Scott and White Health employees and their immediate family members seeking comprehensive financial services
Bell County, Texas residents and business owners looking for a local credit union with community ties
Consumers seeking to combine lending with professional financial planning services in one institution
Members interested in free credit monitoring and financial awareness tools as part of membership
diagnosis: SWECU is best for Baylor Scott and White Health employees and their families, or Bell County residents seeking comprehensive credit union services with integrated financial planning. The main caveat is restricted membership eligibility—most consumers outside the defined service area or without employer affiliation cannot join, and those who can may face geographic limitations for in-person service access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Sherwin Williams Emp. — NCUA-insured credit union in South Holland, IL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
